programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle heads to the island of Tenerife to visit young couple Emma and Edo, whose ambition to rescue a few horses created a very different wild sanctuary. He discovers how this Italian and Brit met and ended up with hundreds of animals on this barren and rocky patch of the Canary Islands. He learns that the couple's dream grew quickly into a haven for more than just their animals and that life on their community farm is also a multi-generational family affair. Ben finds himself knee-deep in the daily realities of 'finca' life and begins to understand what draws volunteers to this place and to its extraordinary hosts. Ben experiences first-hand what life is like for the pair as young parents, where their dreams of running an eco-farm, have turned into running an entire village. Exec Prod: Natalie Wilkinson; Dir: Jo Young; Rushlight Films (S21 Ep5)
Ben Fogle travels round the world to visit people who have given up the rat race.

programme.title: William & Catherine: Putting Family First
programme.desc: This revelatory film explores how the Prince and Princess of Wales are revolutionising the royal family by prioritising their family as well as their duty to the crown.

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Traditions
programme.desc: The tradition of the 'Heir and a Spare' is a deep rooted. We delve into a turbulent history to ask why every monarch is expected to produce at least two offspring - preferably male. It's a custom born out of centuries of high infant mortality and bloody warfare, when having a backup heir was essential. But it can come at a cost to family relations. We also look at how the royals have traditionally never been shy of spending a fortune on a token of love. But that tradition can go too far... Other traditions explored include the curious custom of the royal warrants - issued to everyone from cornflake manufacturers to broadband suppliers- an endorsement worth millions of pounds to many British manufacturers. The show also looks at the traditionally uneasy relationship between monarchs and the aristocracy. Prod/Dir: Simon Newton; Series Prod/Dir: Michael Douglas; Exec Prod: Oliver Wright (S2 Ep4)
Revealing how the monarchy uses traditions to maintain a place in our nation's identity.

programme.title: Crime of Passion? The Trial of Jane Andrews
programme.desc: It was a trial that shocked the nation. In the dock, former dresser and personal aide to the Duchess of York, now accused of brutally murdering her millionaire boyfriend in a fit of jealousy and rage. Jane Andrews claims self-defence. A trial will decide who is right and who is wrong. The man Jane Andrews stabbed to death, was 39-year-old former stockbroker Thomas Cressman. With privileged access to the prosecution and those who witnessed every twist and turn, we tell the story of a trial which would drive a wedge through London's privileged set. Will the jury believe that Andrews was a victim in an abusive relationship and acquit her? Can her defence team prove she was unwell at the time of the murder? Or is Jane Andrews guilty of pre-meditated murder? Prod/Dir: Tom Peppiatt; Exec Prod: Elliot Reed; Good TV

programme.desc: Alexis Conran and his team take on the scammers who come after us when we're simply trying to look after ourselves. programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: In Holywood, County Down, a piece of land has been transformed into an edible forest garden shaped by patience, dedication, and care for wildlife. Toby Demeuldre, who became deeply interested in ecology and rewilding after surviving a childhood brain tumour, has spent years restoring the land-digging ponds, creating habitats, and building a photography hide to observe the animals that now rely on it. With the support of his mother Jane-Louise, the garden stands as both a sanctuary for local wildlife and a deeply personal space of healing, showing how slowing down and nurturing nature can benefit us all. Exec Prods: Fintan Maguire/Catriona Doyle; High Rock Media (S1 Ep11)
Short films focussing on the people, places and projects protecting and restoring nature.

programme.title: The Motorway
programme.desc: The patrollers deal with a freak accident and a family in shock when a lorry's wheel comes off and destroys their car. Heavy rain sees floods, blocked drains and a high-speed aquaplaning disaster on the M42.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Following the work of the Firearms Squad. The team respond to reports of a shotgun changing hands in the suburbs. When the team swoops in, both buyer and seller are taken by surprise.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Humberside Police get a 999 call from a member of the public following an HGV which is all over the road and driving very dangerously. In a dramatic live relay of events, the truck narrowly misses other cars and completely misses a roundabout. Can they stop it before it causes catastrophe? In Cleethorpes, a routine red light stop raises deeper suspicions - thedriver claims this was just an innocent mistake and she's never been in trouble before, but the only problem is that her car stinks of cannabis. Series Prod: Johnny Perks; Exec Prods: Zoë Callan/Dympna Jackson; Dirs: Sam Knowles/John Nichols/Phil Hirst/Emma Fraser; Purple Productions (S9 Ep3)
Ride along with the officers risking their lives policing Britain's most dangerous roads.

programme.title: The Curfew
programme.desc: Six-part crime thriller set in a society where all men live under The Women's Safety act, meaning they are bound by a strict curfew from 7pm to 7am every night, with their movements tracked 24 hours a day by an ankle tag. When a woman is brutally murdered in the night, maverick Detective Pamela Green is convinced only a man could have been responsible for the murder. Her theory is discredited among her peers: in a world where men are under curfew at night and their every move tracked by an ankle tag, it's not possible. Pamela, reluctantly partnered with eager rookie DC Eddie Wilson, sets about finding what happened to the victim. Starring Sarah Parish, Mandip Gill, Alexandra Burke, Lucy Benjmain and Anita Dobson. (Originally screened on Paramount+ with the title Curfew.) (Continues tomorrow.) (Ep1/6)

programme.title: Platoon 24/7: Preparing For War
programme.desc: It's summer in Bulford and 13 Platoon is reaching the end of its training. But ahead of them are the two biggest tests of their six months of training. First is Exercise Iron Storm, in which the platoon fight alongside tanks and artillery in a large-scale exercise across the Salisbury Plain Training Area. With their rifles and body armour equipped with a laser tracking system, this is the first time the platoon can kill - and be killed - in a battle simulation. Their second challenge is an endurance competition, staged on the inhospitable terrain of Dartmoor in Devon. Carrying 30kg of kit, the platoon must race non-stop over 45 kilometres while also undertaking a series of energy-sapping combat exercises. To pass their training the whole platoon must cross the finish line together. Can Platoon Commander Lieutenant Tom Drew and Corporal Jack Sherborne galvanise their soldiers one final time to get them across the finish line together as a band of brothers? (This is the 3rd and final - but retitled - episode of the series Platoon 24/7: Preparing for War.) Exec Prods: Martin Oxley/Jamie Balment; Dir: Will Lorimer; Frank Films and Motion Entertainment Production (S1 Ep3/3)
We follow six gruelling months as a platoon prepares for possible frontline deployment.

programme.title: Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time
programme.desc: St Bartholomew's Hospital is one of the most extraordinary institutions in the world: the oldest hospital in Britain still operating on its original site, and today a global leader in cutting-edge medicine. This series has unprecedented access to both its modern wards and its private archive - a unique collection spanning nearly 1,000 years of medical history, unseen anywhere else. Leading the series is Professor Alice Roberts. From brutal early surgery to robot-assisted operations, she reveals how centuries of risk and innovation shaped modern medicine. In this episode she heads to Barts' extraordinary archive, which holds almost 900 years of fascinating and priceless artefacts; we follow the team as they spring into action for an urgent cardiac emergency; explore the grisly history of x-rays; and visit The Old Operating Theatre where a 1600s manual, The Surgeon's Mate, prompts a re-enactment of the gruesome amputation of a poor woman's gangrenous limbs. Exec Prod: Sarah Sarkhel; Series Prod: Giulia Clark; Lion TV (Ep1)

programme.title: The Surgeon
programme.desc: For the first time, this series reveals exactly what life is really like as a surgeon: from the high pressure world of the operating theatre to the consulting room where news must be broken (both good and bad), to the everyday ups and downs of life at home. Each episode follows a different surgeon as they juggle these very different worlds, making life-changing decisions on a daily basis. It's a dramatic and deeply personal series. In this first episode we follow Daren Francis, one of the UK's leading bowel cancer surgeons, who works across three different hospitals in north London. Among his patients are retired nurse Doris, who is in great pain with a tumour that he suspects is cancerous and blocking her bowel; and 67-year-old Antonio, to whom Daren must break the news that he has a lump in his bowel which is cancer. Series Prod: Freddie Foss-Smith; Exec Prod: David Granger; Dirs: Kat Bannon/Anna Davis; idle Industries (Ep1/4)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Suffolk Strangler
programme.desc: On 3rd February 2026, former forklift driver Steve Wright pleaded guilty to the murder of Victoria Hall, 27 years after the 17-year-old was found dead after going missing in 1999. Over a two-week period in December 2006, five sex workers were found dead, their bodies dumped in rural locations outside Ipswich. Suffolk police launched their biggest ever manhunt, for a suspected serial killer on the loose - and on the rampage. A DNA match led police to Steve Wright, who would go on to be known as the Suffolk Strangler, yet there's a side to this story that you've not heard before. In this feature-length drama-documentary we go inside the courtroom. With privileged access to both the prosecution and to Wright's defence, for the first time we reveal their battle in the courtroom. And we examine the crucial evidence of the forensic experts, who played a major role in the outcome of the trial. Prod/Dir: Tom Harrad; Exec Prod: Elliot Reed; Good TV
